name: Dawn's Way and Safor Loop
name_original: La Vía Verde del Serpis — Circular desde Lorxa
description: This difficult 35.6-mile mountain biking route follows the Serpis river through a stunning gorge, featuring old railway tunnels and bridges.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lorcha/Orxa offers diverse terrain within the Comtat region of the Valencian Community. The area features hilly, Mediterranean landscapes characterized by rugged surfaces, loose stones, and ruts. Trails often traverse unspoiled valleys, orange groves, and sections alongside the Serpis River, providing varied challenges for riders. This region is known for its natural beauty and traditional Spanish character, making it a compelling destination for outdoor activities. Best mountain bike trails around Lorcha/Orxa The most popular mountain bike trail is Dawn's Way and Safor Loop, a 35.6 miles (57.3 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Serpis River – Azud del Infierno or del Forn loop from Castell de Benissili, a difficult 26.3 miles (42.4 km) path. This trail follows the Serpis River, offering challenging sections and scenic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Fábrica de l'Infern – Serpis River loop from l'Orxa / Lorcha, a 13.1 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through the Serpis River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Lorcha/Orxa is defined by rugged terrain, hilly Mediterranean landscapes, and routes along the Serpis River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Lorcha/Orxa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lorcha/Orxa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails are available in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Lorcha/Orxa offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 5 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 27 difficult routes. The terrain is typically rugged, featuring loose stones and ruts, often traversing hilly Mediterranean landscapes, orange groves, and sections alongside the Serpis River.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Yes, there are mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. The region offers 5 easy routes, providing a good starting point for those new to mountain biking or looking for a less challenging ride. These trails allow riders to enjoy the scenic Mediterranean landscapes at a comfortable pace.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Lorcha/Orxa boasts 27 difficult routes. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924784561/dawn-s-way-and-safor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dawn's Way and Safor Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35.6 miles with over 1,490 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Lorcha/Orxa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10351484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serpis River – Azud del Infierno or del Forn loop from Castell de Benissili&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38206639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fábrica de l'Infern – Serpis River loop from l'Orxa / Lorcha&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient options for your ride.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: The trails often lead through areas of significant natural beauty. You might encounter the dramatic Barranco del Infierno (Hell's Gorge), ride alongside the Serpis River, or witness the unique natural arch of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views of the Vall de la Gallinera. Other notable spots include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2897664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l’Estret&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4053196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud del Infierno or del Forn&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with historical elements. You can ride past the renovated Old Lorcha Railway Station, which is now part of a greenway, or explore the charming Forna village with its impressive Castillo de Forna, a fortified palace from the late Middle Ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4053196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud del Infierno or del Forn&lt;/a&gt; also represents a historical man-made structure integrated into the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate, the best times for mountain biking in Lorcha/Orxa are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, allowing you to fully enjoy the trails without the intense heat of summer.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take around Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38206639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fábrica de l'Infern – Serpis River loop from l'Orxa / Lorcha&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes (13.1 miles). Longer, difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924784561/dawn-s-way-and-safor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dawn's Way and Safor Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 5 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied and challenging terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Mediterranean landscapes, and the scenic routes along the Serpis River. The mix of rugged paths and historical points of interest also contributes to a memorable riding experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for a half-day trip in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Yes, there are several mountain bike routes perfect for a half-day adventure. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38206639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fábrica de l'Infern – Serpis River loop from l'Orxa / Lorcha&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.1-mile trail that typically takes around 1 hour 32 minutes, making it an excellent option for a shorter ride.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect while mountain biking in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: You can expect a beautiful and varied landscape characterized by hilly, Mediterranean terrain. The routes often wind through unspoiled valleys, vibrant orange groves, and rugged sections with rocky surfaces. The presence of the Serpis River adds another scenic dimension, with trails following its course through gorges and natural areas.
tips: Old train station where the beautiful Serpis Greenway now passes. The Greenway uses the old railway structure that linked Alcoy with Gandía, built by the English. The one known as the "English train" was created at the end of the 19th century. The reason was the growth of the industry in the city of Alcoi. The Serpis attracted textile factories, paper mills and mills to its banks. These took advantage of the force of its flow to generate energy. Since 1969 the line stopped working. Recently, this route is part of the Serpis Greenway.
Very nice views of both Benicadell and the reservoir. Both on one side and the other you can ride on good roads and appreciate beautiful views
Along this section of the path you will have some very nice panoramic views. It is a short section that you can extend both towards Lorxa and Montichelvo. It is very fun and entertaining to shoot
Panoramic site to the Serpis river and the surroundings of the Greenway
Very showy weir on one side of the VV del Serpis

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the "La Vía Verde del Serpis — Circular desde Lorxa" route is rated as difficult. While sections of the Vía Verde del Serpis are generally accessible, this specific circular route incorporates more demanding terrain, including loose stone, and significant elevation gain, requiring good to very good fitness.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is estimated to take approximately 5 hours and 21 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery and historical points.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of natural beauty and historical structures. Highlights include the Old Lorcha Railway Station, stunning Views of Benicadell, and the picturesque Cami de Las Fontetes. You'll also pass the Serpis River Weir and the historic Azud del Infierno or del Forn. The route is also known for its numerous tunnels, bridges, and remnants of the old Alcoy-Gandía railway line.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Lorxa?
faqs.answer: Specific parking facilities for the "Circular desde Lorxa" route are typically available near the old railway station in L'Orxa (Lorcha), which serves as a common starting point for the Vía Verde del Serpis. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the La Vía Verde del Serpis — Circular desde Lorxa mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the Vía Verde del Serpis. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times, especially given the presence of other cyclists, hikers, and potential wildlife. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for mountain biking. Summer can be very hot, and some sections might be less enjoyable due to the heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the trail or the Paratge Natural Municipal Penyes Albes?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no permits or entrance fees required to access the Vía Verde del Serpis or to mountain bike within the Paratge Natural Municipal Penyes Albes. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the circular route from Lorxa?
faqs.answer: While the core Vía Verde section follows an old railway line with gentle slopes, the circular route from Lorxa includes more varied and challenging terrain. Expect sections with loose stone, unpaved paths, and significant climbs and descents. Some parts may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Camino del Alba and also passes through sections of the l'Orxa - Àrees Recreatives and El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain biking trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and duration, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a first-aid kit, and a repair kit for your bike. A headlamp or strong bike light is highly recommended for the numerous tunnels, some of which are quite long and dark. Always wear appropriate safety gear, including a helmet.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation options nearby in Lorxa?
faqs.answer: Lorxa (L'Orxa) is a small town, but you can typically find some basic amenities, including cafes or small restaurants, especially near the town center. For accommodation, it's best to check local guesthouses or rural accommodations in advance, particularly if you plan an overnight stay.

waypoints.name: Azud del Infierno or del Forn
